sections.paragraph:
νεφρός, οῦ, ὁ (Aristoph., Pla. et al.; LXX; OdeSol 11:2; TestSol; ApcrEzk P 1 recto 7 [Denis p. 125]; Philo; Jos., Ant. 3, 228) usu. pl. ‘kidneys’; by fig. ext. (cp. the use of σπλάγχνον), of the inner life mind (LXX). Of the Human One (Son of Man) ἐρευνῶν νεφροὺς καὶ καρδίας who searches minds and hearts (Diod S 1, 91, 5 νεφροὶ καὶ καρδία are the only things left in the body cavity by the Egyptian embalmers) Rv 2:23 (in the OT a similar expr., w. different verbs, is used of God: Ps 7:10; Jer 11:20; 17:10; 20:12).—DELG s.v. νεφροί. M-M. TW.

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

OdeSol OdeSol = Gk. Ode of Solomon 11: PBodmer XI; III a.d.—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

ApcrEzk ApcrEzk = Apocryphon Ezechiel—List 2

Denis Denis = AD., Fragmenta pseudepigraphorum quae supersunt Graeca—Lists 2 beg., 5, 6

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

usu. usu. = usually

  1. pl. = plural

ext. ext. = extension

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

OT OT = Old Testament

expr. expr. = expression

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 670
